title: ZeHethbra
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Youmay|the species|the [[Zehethbra]] [[Language/Legends|language]]}}

{{Quote|I told you what I'd do! Time to party!|Raije|<ref name="ND">[[No Disintegrations (West End Games)|''No Disintegrations'']]</ref>}}
The '''ZeHethbra''' were furry, [[Humanoid/Legends|anthropomorphic]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] with vestigial tail, claws and [[Tooth/Legends|fangs]]. They possessed an acute sense of smell that they could use to detect their fellow ZeHethbra's [[Pheromone/Legends|pheromones]] and thus gather information about them. They could secrete a natural biochemical spray to blind and stun foes in a fight, although other ZeHethbra were immune to its effects. ZeHethbra were moody and bad-tempered, and valued honesty and social status. As they were aggressive and strong, they commonly found employment in security forces, military organizations, or related fields, such as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]].

The ZeHethbra evolved on the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[ZeHeth/Legends|ZeHeth]], joining the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] millennia before the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The species founded settlements on several planets, giving rise to autonomous ZeHethbra cultural groups. While trying to establish a colony on [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]], they waged a [[Dug–ZeHethbra war|war]] against the [[Dug/Legends|Dugs]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|That son of a bantha has more warrants for his arrest than I got fur on my face.|Clyngunn|<ref name="Underworld">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref>}}
[[File:Clyngunn_Gamer.png|thumb|left|upright|A white stripe goes from the snout of a ZeHethbra]]
The ZeHethbra were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], robust, strong [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who reached the considerable height<ref name="AE" /> of 1.90 to 2.30 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in adulthood.<ref name="UA" /> Their athletic constitution provided them with a great physical power.<ref name="AE" /> They retained vestigial [[Tooth/Legends|fangs]] in their mouths and small claws on their paw-like hands; although these were ineffective in combat, they were useful when threatening members of other species.<ref name="UA" /> Each of the hands had three fingers plus one opposable thumb.<ref name="hand">As seen in the images from ''[[Alien Encounters]]'', "[[The Smugglers Alliance]]," and ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref> A ZeHethbra had raised, triangular ears. Some ZeHethbra [[Piercing/Legends|pierced]] these organs to attach one<ref name="TLCSB">''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]''</ref> or more earrings to them.<ref name="ND">[[No Disintegrations (West End Games)|''No Disintegrations'']]</ref> ZeHethbra retained a short tail from their evolutionary past.<ref name="AE" /> In the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the species was fully bipedal and had no need for a tail;<ref name="G3">{{GamerCite|3|The Smugglers Alliance}}</ref> indeed, they could run as fast as a [[Human/Legends|Human]].<ref name="UA" />

ZeHethbra were [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]],<ref name="AE 182" /> and each was covered in short fur with one predominant [[Color/Legends|color]] that depended on the place of birth: ZeHethbra from the south pole of their [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[ZeHeth/Legends|ZeHeth]], had white-blue fur, while those from the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] in the northern hemisphere were brown or red. Crossbreeds could show spots or mottling.<ref name="AE" /> Coat colors ranged from black to mottled brown.<ref name="UA" /> No matter the main color, a ZeHethbra always had a stripe of white fur that started at the snout, passed over the top of the head, thickened as it went down the back, until it reached the tail.<ref name="AE" /><ref name="UA" /> This stripe was narrower in [[Gender/Legends|females]], and males' stripes branched while females' did not.<ref name="AE" /> ZeHethbra also commonly had three long whiskers at each side of the snout.<ref name="whisker">As seen in the images from ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'', ''[[Alien Encounters]]'', "[[The Smugglers Alliance]]," and ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''.</ref>

The ZeHethbra snout gave the species an acute sense of smell. They were known to produce sophisticated, natural [[Pheromone/Legends|pheromones]] that identified the individual's origins, although only another ZeHethbra could interpret that information from such a smell.<ref name="AE" /> Thus, the cocktail of biochemical pheromones secreted by a ZeHethbra served as a unique identifier of that individual.<ref name="UA" />

In moments of danger<ref name="AE" /> or rage, a ZeHethbra was able to generate a powerful<ref name="UA" /> biochemical spray<ref name="UA" /><ref name="G3" /> that could be used to disorient, sting,<ref name="UA" /> stun,<ref name="AE" /><ref name="UA" /> and blind any opponent hit by it.<ref name="AE" /> This spray affected any organic being<ref name="G3" />—except for other ZeHethbra<ref name="UA" />—in a three-<ref name="AE" /><ref name="ND" /><ref name="G3" /> to four-meter radius. Weaker victims were known to suffer from a severe nausea that prevented them from counterattacking,<ref name="UA" /> and some targets even collapsed temporarily.<ref name="AE" /><ref name="ND" />

With a life expectancy of eighty-five [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard years]], the ZeHethbra started their adolescence at ten years of age. Reaching biological maturity at sixteen, they did not suffer the ill effects of age before forty-five. Even then they rarely had to retire before sixty-five.<ref name="UA" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|I don't like competition. I don't like you. The Devaronian girl is mine. The bounty is mine. Stay out of my way. If I see you again, I shoot you on sight!|Raije|<ref name="ND" />}}
[[File:Raije.png|thumb|right|upright|ZeHethbra were moody and aggressive.]]
The ZeHethbra grouped themselves into clans and used pheromones as the basis for most of their cultural identity. A person's pheromones identified unambiguously that individual's clan and, as a consequence, his or her family history.<ref name="AE" /> The ZeHethbra were known to value honesty<ref name="TT">Information repeated in ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' and ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref> and social status, and they showed a great deal of respect for people with leadership qualities.<ref name="UA" />

When on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], ZeHeth, the ZeHethbra lived either in sprawling urban complexes or in rural lairs that they built among the roots of humongous [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. The ZeHethbra disliked violators of personal space, so they fiercely protected their living areas, families, clans and territories from trespassers.<ref name="UA" /> Although they were covered in fur, ZeHethbra donned clothing to cover their hairy bodies.<ref name="AE" />

ZeHethbra were temperamental beings, prone to attack verbally or physically anyone who caused them discomfort. The common ZeHethbra response to a perceived adversary was to growl and even spit. Should the target of such ire be another ZeHethbra, he or she joined the instigator in an aggressive snorting contest. Occasionally, agitated ZeHethbra violently attacked a perceived foe with what other species considered little provocation; in fact, a ZeHethbra might even pounce upon a physically superior opponent.<ref name="UA" /> Such attacks sometimes involved the ZeHethbra's natural spray as well.<ref name="PID">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030417positiveid|text=Positive ID|int=Positive ID}}.</ref> Although these outbursts rarely ended in [[Death/Legends|death]], many a young ZeHethbra met his or her match, and fate, in this way.<ref name="UA" />

The ZeHethbra had an official [[Language/Legends|language]], known also as [[Zehethbra]], which most members of the species learned to speak and write. To a layperson's ear, this seemed to be formed of gurgles, gnarls and grunts uttered in a tantrum. Many ZeHethbra learned to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] as their second language. A ZeHethbra commonly had a single-word name, such as Fyntarr, Rygulan or Sarnizak.<ref name="UA" /> At least one [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-roaming individual added the epithet "the ZeHethbra" after his one-word moniker.<ref name="L10">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|The alert's out. All our ships and ground stations, and I got word to Mazzic's and Clyngunn's people, too. If Lak Jit gets near any of them, we'll have him.|[[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] mentioning Clyngunn the ZeHethbra|<ref name="SotP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>}}
[[File:ZeHethbra-AE.png|thumb|left|upright|ZeHethbra used common technology]]
The ZeHethbra evolved on the temperate<ref name="UA" /> planet ZeHeth,<ref name="AE" /><ref name="UA" /> in the [[Dustig sector/Legends|Dustig sector]]<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="UA" /> ZeHeth was between the nearby [[Pax system/Legends|Pax system]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]], and the [[Malastare system/Legends|Malastare system]], also in the Mid Rim.<ref name="EA" /> In their past, the ZeHethbra developed a number of adaptive traits that eventually lost their original functions through evolution, but remained in the civilized species in vestigial form. These phenotypes included the tail,<ref name="AE" /> fangs, and claws, although the latter were still used for intimidation.<ref name="UA" />

The ZeHethbra first came into contact with the Galactic Republic at some point between [[8000 BBY]] and [[5500 BBY/Legends|5500 BBY]].<ref name="EA" /> From that moment on, the species traveled through the galaxy and settled on several planets, creating disparate racial, cultural and ethnic groups<ref name="AE" /><ref name="UA" /> due to the differences in geography and history. Each group developed its own distinctions, including body language, skin markings, pheromones and linguistic accent. The ZeHethbra recognized no fewer than eighty such groups during the times of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], but these sub-units were too similar for non-ZeHethbra to recognize more than six of them.<ref name="AE" />

In [[3700 BBY/Legends|3700 BBY]], the galaxy-crossing [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] known as the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] was created with ZeHeth falling along its path. This allowed the ZeHethbra to move more easily throughout the galaxy.<ref name="EA" /> At some point before [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], the ZeHethbra founded several colonies in the Malastare system. The native [[Dug/Legends|Dugs]], a species prone to anger and violence, disliked their new neighbors. This led to [[Dug–ZeHethbra war|open war]] between the ZeHethbra and the Dugs. There was no clear winner in this conflict but, eventually, the Galactic Republic disarmed the Dugs; the conflict with the ZeHethbra was one contributing factor precipitating this sanction.<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' {{C|Under ''Dug''}}</ref>

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the ZeHethbra homeworld was in the territory belonging to the Galactic Republic, as opposed to that controlled by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Circa [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], during the first stages of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the population of ZeHeth was in the billions.<ref name="EA" />

==ZeHethbra in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Now there's someone I wouldn't want to meet twice. Crazy as a dinko and twice as mean. His name is Raije and he's some kind of rogue ZeHethbra.|[[Malric Blaene]]|<ref name="ND" />}}
[[File:Clygunn_TLC.jpg|thumb|right|upright|ZeHethbra joined the galactic community]]
The ZeHethbra were a technologically advanced<ref name="UA" /> well-known species in the outer galaxy. Being muscled, quick, and intelligent,<ref name="AE" /> the ZeHethbra were in great demand as guards and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. They worked for either governments or criminals, and members of the species were commonly found in military jobs. A few [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] ZeHethbra followed the path of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and became [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]].<ref name="UA" />

The [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] known as [[Raije]] traveled through the galaxy in search of suitable prey. At the beginning of his career, he used a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]], allegedly taken from the corpse of his first victim, as his weapon of choice. Raije was seen on [[Abregado-rae/Legends|Abregado-rae]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="ND" />

[[Clyngunn]], later known as Clyngunn the ZeHethbra, became a renowned athlete by winning the [[Multi-Sentient Unarmed Combat Rounds]] in the [[Stratis Games]] of [[Hallrin IV]]. After retiring from sports, he became a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] working for the veteran smuggler, [[Billey]]. Clyngunn eventually became a smuggling legend in his own right, with a justified reputation for working alone—indeed he was the only crewman of his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Lady Sunfire]]''. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], he was approached by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], who wanted to ensure that Clyngunn would keep the neutral smuggler's stance of supporting neither the Empire nor the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Clyngunn later heard from [[Talon Karrde]], who wanted important smugglers like the ZeHethbra to actively support the New Republic. Eventually, Clyngunn joined Karrde's [[Smugglers' Alliance]], and he was still an active member [[19 ABY/Legends|ten years later]], helping during the [[Caamas Document Crisis]].<ref name="clyngunn">Clyngunn is featured in ''[[The Last Command]]'' and issues [[The Last Command 2|#2]], [[The Last Command 3|#3]], and [[The Last Command 4|#4]] of [[Star Wars: The Last Command (comic series)|its comic adaptation]]; ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]''; ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''; ''[[Specter of the Past]]''; ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''; {{GamerCite|3|The Smugglers Alliance}}; {{GamerCite|4|Ships of the Smuggler's Alliance}}; and {{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ClyngunnTallGuy.jpg|thumb|left|Clyngunn, far left, towers above smugglers of other species.]]
The ZeHethbra first appeared in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s [[1993]] novel ''[[The Last Command]]'', mostly through the character of Clyngunn.<ref name="L10" /> The species was given a background in the [[1994]] role-playing book ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Eric S. Trautmann]], edited by [[Bill Smith]] and adapted to [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="TLCSB" /> That book's text about Clyngunn and the ZeHethbra was repeated verbatim in ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' ([[1996]])<ref name="TSB">''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref> and once more, with only slight variations ("three-meter" instead of "three meter" and "venom spray" instead of "spray"), in ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' ([[1998]]).<ref name="AE 182" /> While the first two books included a section about Clyngunn,<ref name="TLCSB" /><ref name="L10" /> ''Alien Encounters'' did not.<ref name="AE 182" />

These roleplaying books specify that adult ZeHethbra can reach a height between 1.60 and 1.80 meters, describing them as "tall," but the later book ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' ([[2003]])<ref name="UA" /> says that the common height for an adult ZeHethbra is 190 to 230 centimeters, which is more consistent with Clyngunn's height as seen in [[Star Wars: The Last Command (comic series)|the comic adaptation of ''The Last Command'']].<ref name="TLC2">[[The Last Command 2|''The Last Command'' 2]]</ref>

The sources detailing the species, including ''The Last Command Sourcebook'',<ref name="TLCSB" /> ''The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook'',<ref name="TSB" /> ''Alien Encounters''<ref name="AE 182" /> and ''Ultimate Alien Anthology'', invariably mention the white streak in the ZeHethbra's fur. Curiously, the ZeHethbra illustration in ''Ultimate Alien Anthology'' lacks any visible white fur for unexplained reasons.<ref name="UA" /> The article "[[The Smugglers Alliance]]" published in [[Star Wars Gamer 3|''Star Wars Gamer'' #3]] ([[2001]]) lists Clyngunn as a ZeHethbra but, due to a typo, his spray is listed as a "[[Defel/Legends|Defel]]" special ability.<ref name="G3" />
